raw_transcript: If we don't see a significant drop in stool frequency within 72 hours, we'll perform a 'rescue' therapy. That usually means either Infliximab or Cyclosporine. We want to avoid a colectomy, which is the surgical removal of the colon, at all costs.
final_corrected_note: If we don't see a significant drop in stool frequency within 72 hours, we'll perform a 'rescue' therapy. That usually means either Infliximab or Cyclosporine. We want to avoid a colectomy, which is the surgical removal of the colon, at all costs.
